business.headline: Are you eligible for additional UPS benefits? Here's what govt saysReplying to a question in the Lok Sabha, Minister of State for Finance Pankaj Chaudhary said that 25,756 retired Central government subscribers are eligible to claim additional benefits under the UPS.

Volkswagen weighs JSW partnership for India businessVolkswagen is in advanced talks with JSW Group over an investment in Skoda Auto Volkswagen India. The proposed deal could reshape the carmaker's India strategy as competition intensifies.

Everyone wants AI engineers. But who will make sure AI actually works?As companies increasingly deploy AI across banking, healthcare, customer service and other sectors, another talent shortage may be quietly emerging; professionals who can test whether these systems are accurate, secure and reliable before they reach users.

Maruti Suzuki announces fresh price hike: Check how much more you'll have to payMaruti Suzuki will raise vehicle prices by up to Rs 30,000 from August. The move marks its second portfolio-wide increase in about two months amid sustained cost pressures.

Can ITR portal handle the filing rush before July 31? Here's what government saysIn a written reply in Parliament, the government said that while some users may experience occasional delays during periods of heavy traffic, the portal has remained largely stable despite a significant increase in the number of taxpayers using it this year.

RBI's foreign currency deposit scheme for NRIs explainedThe RBI has rolled out a concessional swap facility to revive FCNR(B) deposits after a sharp fall in inflows. The move could help banks raise rates, but NRIs still need to weigh currency needs and taxes.

Dalal Street ends lower on HDFC Bank drag; midcaps continue to outperformThe BSE Sensex closed 238.41 points, or 0.31%, lower at 77,470.11, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 50.80 points, or 0.21%, to settle at 24,187.70.

Gift Deed vs Sale Deed vs Will: Here's how to pick the right way to transfer propertyA single document can decide how smoothly your property changes hands and whether your family faces disputes later. So, should you opt for a Gift Deed, Sale Deed or a Will?

India's biggest private banks are quietly reducing their workforce. Why?HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank and Axis Bank ended FY26 with more than 13,000 fewer employees. The decline reflects attrition, redeployment and technology-led productivity rather than mass layoffs.

Who does Warren Buffett credit for his extraordinary success?Buffett bought his first shares at the age of 11, purchasing three shares of Cities Service for $38 each. That small investment marked the beginning of a career that would eventually turn him into one of the world's most successful investors.

SBI Funds Management lists at nearly 7% premium: Should you buy, sell or hold?SBI Funds Management listed at a modest premium after its heavily subscribed IPO. The debut beat the issue price but fell short of grey market expectations, leaving investors split on booking gains or holding.

Why are farmers marching to Delhi? Key reasons behind the protestWhile farmer unions have paused their next move after Haryana assured talks with the Centre and the release of detained leaders, they remain firm in opposing the proposed India-US trade deal.
Sensex, Nifty open lower as HDFC Bank drags; IT stocks cap lossesThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 54.36 points to 77,654.16, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 8.70 points to 24,229.80 as of 9:25 am.

SBI Funds Management IPO to list today: Will it open strongly? Check GMPSBI Funds Management will make its market debut on Tuesday after strong IPO demand. Grey market trends and institutional buying have kept expectations of healthy listing gains alive.

Will India raise ethanol blending beyond 20%? Here's what the Minister saysResponding to concerns about vehicle performance, the Minister said the government has not received any widespread or verified complaints from vehicle manufacturers, automobile associations or consumer groups regarding engine failure, fuel pump problems, corrosion or water contamination linked to E20 fuel.

Dalal Street ends lower on weak bank earnings, rising Middle East tensionsThe BSE Sensex fell 442.93 points, or 0.57%, to close at 77,708.52. The NSE Nifty50 also ended in the red, slipping 95.80 points, or 0.39%, to settle at 24,238.50.

Government says no proposal to scrap LTCG tax on equitiesThe government has ruled out scrapping the long-term capital gains tax on listed equities, Finance MoS Pankaj Chaudhary told the Lok Sabha. The government also said LTCG tax collections rose 78% year-on-year, highlighting its growing contribution to revenue.

Rising crude prices hit fuel margins. Why are OMC profits still holding up?Despite weaker fuel marketing margins, integrated profits, which combine refining and fuel marketing performance, remained well above recent averages.

SBI Funds Management IPO lists tomorrow with 18% GMP: Is it a long-term buy?SBI Funds Management is all set to debut on the stock exchanges tomorrow, backed by a strong 18% grey market premium. But does that make it a good long-term investment? Let's have a look.

EPFO pension stopped? Submit Digital Life Certificate from home in 6 easy stepsHas your EPFO pension stopped unexpectedly? One reason could be that your Digital Life Certificate (Jeevan Pramaan) has not been submitted. Here's how to complete the process from home in six easy steps.

entertainment.headline: Shabana Azmi suffers asthma attack during CJP protest, says tear gas triggered it Shabana Azmi suffered an asthma attack after tear gas was used at her during the CJP protest in Delhi. She said she returned after recovering, backed the students during the march.

Godzilla x Kong actor Kaylee Hottle dies in car accident at 18 Kaylee Hottle, who played Jia in the Godzilla films, has died at 18 after a car accident in Maryland. Her father confirmed the news on Facebook.

If she's with me, my instinct is to protect her: Raghav on shielding Shehnaz Gill Raghav Juyal addressed the viral birthday clip showing him shielding Shehnaaz Gill through a crowd outside the venue. He said his reaction was instinctive, rooted in his upbringing, and not about appearances.

Exclusive: Matt Damon recalls the risk that changed his and Ben Affleck's lives Matt Damon and Ben Affleck's four-decade friendship has remained a constant through their personal and professional highs and lows. The duo also risked losing Good Will Hunting rather than compromise on their creative vision, a gamble that eventually led to Oscar success.

I don't get bothered: Karanvir Bohra reacts to divorce rumours with wife Teejay Actor Karanvir Bohra has responded to rumours about his marriage with Teejay Sidhu. The two have been married for a decade now.

Annu Kapoor questions SRK's 'last superstar' tag, asks if stardom can last till 2045 Annu Kapoor has disputed Shah Rukh Khan's 'last of the stars' claim, comparing the actor's career with the enduring legacy of Amitabh Bachchan and other industry legends.

I saw young girls being lathi-charged: Shabana Azmi on CJP Delhi protest In an exclusive chat with India Today, actor Shabana Azmi said she saw tear gas and lathi charge being used on students during the Sansad Chalo march. She said the protesters stayed non-violent and refused to back down despite injuries.

Not divisive: Jana Nayagan director agrees Vijay's film has 'direct' politics Director H Vinoth says Jana Nayagan includes direct politics but does not target any person or party. In an exclusive interview with us, he says the Vijay film is rooted in democracy, unity and standing with the people.

Their concerns deserve to be heard: Elvish Yadav backs students' protest in Delhi YouTuber Elvish Yadav criticised the use of force on students during the Chalo Sansad protest over NEET-UG. His remarks came after clashes erupted as security personnel stopped the march to Parliament.

Avengers Doomsday trailer X review: Poor VFX faces flak, fans compare it to Ramayana Marvel has released the first full trailer for Avengers: Doomsday, unveiling Robert Downey Jr as Doctor Doom and a crossover of MCU, Fantastic Four and X-Men characters. The footage has prompted sharp fan reactions over its CGI, missing heroes and the surprise return of a young Steve Rogers.

We were scared of mob attack: Kangana on mood inside Parliament during CJP march Actor-politician Kangana Ranaut said MPs inside the Lok Sabha feared CJP protesters might storm Parliament during the Chalo Sansad march. She praised Delhi Police for stopping any untoward incident.

Fixed political agenda in films is restrictive: Imtiaz Ali on being apolitical Filmmaker Imtiaz Ali explained why he calls himself an apolitical filmmaker while discussing Main Vaapas Aaunga. He said the film's politics grows from personal desires, not a fixed ideological agenda.

Rapper Hanumankind marches with protesting students in Delhi, video goes viral Rapper Hanumankind joined students at the Chalo Sansad protest in New Delhi and shared videos from the march. A video of the singer marching alongside the students has gone viral.

Spider-Man: Brand New Day eyes 5 lakh pre-sales in India, beats The Odyssey records Tom Holland's Spider-Man: Brand New Day has created a buzz in India by registering the highest day-one advance bookings among Hollywood films this year, surpassing Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey. With over 1.4 lakh tickets sold a week before release, the film is poised to cross 5 lakh advance sales.

Akshay Kumar was not Priyadarshan's first choice for Haiwaan. Bobby and Akshaye were Priyadarshan said Bobby Deol and Akshaye Khanna were considered before Akshay Kumar joined Haiwaan as the antagonist. The casting turn highlights the film's shift into a cat-and-mouse action thriller.

People only saw me with a cigarette and saree: Mahie Gill on being stereotyped Actor Mahie Gill, in an interview, said how OTT gave her space to move beyond stereotyped glamorous roles. She said the shift has created stronger parts for women and more work across the industry.

Hina Khan, Shilpa Shinde set for explosive Lock Upp 2 reunion? Actor Hina Khan is reportedly set to make a special appearance on Lock Upp 2, where Shilpa Shinde is a contestant. The possible on-screen reunion has renewed attention on their Bigg Boss 11 rivalry and recent public exchanges.

Preity Zinta extends unwavering support to Sonam Wangchuk, protesting students Preity Zinta has backed students protesting in Delhi over NEET irregularities and urged the government to speak to Sonam Wangchuk. Her intervention came as the movement widened after the Chalo Sansad clashes and calls for education reform intensified.

Nandamuri Balakrishna injured on NBK111 sets; Jr NTR, Kalyan Ram react Actor-politician Nandamuri Balakrishna suffered a minor injury while shooting for NBK111 in Kakinada. Jr NTR and Nandamuri Kalyan Ram wished him a speedy recovery on X.

general news.headline: Dramatic scenes as Rahul Gandhi-led protest outside PM residence broken up by copsCongress leaders led by Rahul Gandhi marched to the Prime Minister's residence over the NEET paper leak and alleged police action on students. The protest triggered talks with the Centre, police detentions and a sharper political confrontation in Parliament and on the streets.

Students deserve answers, accountability, not outrage: Pradhan slams CongressDharmendra Pradhan's remarks came hours after Congress leaders, including Rahul Gandhi, staged a protest outside the Prime Minister's residence over the police action against students protesting the NEET paper leak.

From crackdown to comeback: Police action drew more protestors to Jantar MantarJuly 20 marked the day the hunger strike at Jantar Mantar erupted into a mass protest, with thousands demanding the Education Minister's resignation before marching towards Parliament on the opening day of the Monsoon Session. As the crowds dispersed, the scars of the crackdown — and the stories left behind — remained. India Today Digital returned to the protest site the next day to capture what unfolded after the headlines. Here's our report...

Pakistan seeks $10 billion US currency backstop after Iran war diplomacyPakistan has sought a $10 billion exchange stabilisation facility from the United States. The request could ease pressure on reserves and the rupee while signalling closer ties with Washington.
Protester's playbook to bring in real changeHere's a practical guide to a successful protest in a democracy where we elect our MPs, our direct employees.

No debate from govt, so we staged dharna outside PM's residence, says Rahul GandhiRahul Gandhi said the government had no interest in debating students' concerns in Parliament, prompting the Congress to stage a dharna outside the Prime Minister's residence. He demanded resignations, an apology from PM Modi, withdrawal of cases and reforms in the education system.

Canada's Indian-origin foreign minister visits Pakistan, announces millions in aidCanada's Indian-origin foreign minister Anita Anand visited Pakistan, marking the first visit by a Canadian foreign minister in nearly two decades, where she announced 47 million dollars in aid and unveiled new trade, investment and security initiatives as Ottawa seeks to deepen ties with Islamabad despite regional tensions.

Delhi, Gujarat, Maharashtra to witness extreme rain Wednesday, South to stay dryHeavy to extremely heavy rain is likely at isolated places over Gujarat Region and Madhya Maharashtra on Wednesday, July 22, while South Peninsular India stays subdued for a week. Delhi sees two rain spells as hill states face flash flood risk.

Engineering from Chaitanya Bharathi and no IIT, Uday Ruddarraju is now OpenAI CTOOpenAI has a new CTO and he is Uday Ruddarraju with his Hyderabad roots. Interestingly, Uday is not from any IIT or a similarly storied institute. Instead, he finished his engineering from Chaitanya Bharathi Institute of Technology.

Went where they least expected us: Priyanka Gandhi on protest outside PM's housePriyanka Gandhi, addressing reporters after her release from Mandi Marg police station, said the Congress was demanding justice for students affected by the alleged paper leak, a discussion in Parliament and the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan.

India-bound Saudi crude tanker makes U-turn in Red Sea after Houthis' warningA Saudi crude tanker bound for India turned back in the Red Sea after a Houthi warning. It comes after the Houthis declared a naval blockade against Saudi Arabia and warned shipping companies not to load or unload cargo at Saudi ports.

How do I blind them? AI searches reveal Bengaluru triple murder plotPolice allege the murders were meticulously planned, with the main accused, Kenneth, using Gemini to seek indirect advice on attacking and killing. Investigators claim he explored using an iron furnace to dispose of the bodies before abandoning the idea, and believed the killings would free him from financial pressure.

Colder, taller, deeper: Storm clouds cover 70% of India, Isro satellites showIsro's INSAT-3DR and INSAT-3DS satellites show cloud cover crossing 70 per cent of India on July 21, 2026, with storm tops turning colder and taller. Here is what Cloud Top Brightness Temperature reveals about the storms overhead, and what it means for Delhi.

Trump tightens student visa rules: What Indians studying in US should do nowThe Trump administration has proposed replacing duration-of-status for most international students with fixed-term US visas. The shift could force many Indian students to track deadlines more closely and seek extensions sooner.

Live: Jitendra Singh, JP Nadda meet Sonam Wangchuk at Medanta hospitalA day after the Cockroach Janta Party's (CJP) 'Chalo Sansad' march turned violent, the political fallout intensified on Tuesday as Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi and other Congress leaders were detained following a protest outside Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence. The leaders had gathered near the residence and raised slogans against the government over the police action against students during Monday's march. Earlier, Union Minister Jitendra Singh met Rahul Gandhi and other Opposition leaders in an attempt to defuse the situation, but the talks failed, following which police intervened and detained the protesting leaders. Meanwhile, leaders across political parties visited those injured during the clashes. Union Health Minister JP Nadda visited Delhi's Ram Manohar Lohia (RML) Hospital and Lady Hardinge Medical College and Hospital, while Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra met injured students at RML Hospital. Security remains heightened across the national capital, with authorities continuing to monitor the situation following the protests and clashes. Follow India Today for the latest updates.

Video: Gurugram man suffers spinal fractures after rusted basketball pole collapsesA rusted basketball pole collapsed during practice at a Gurugram housing society, critically injuring 22-year-old Kartik. The accident has intensified demands for an FIR and scrutiny of neglected sports infrastructure.
Gen Zs protested at home before making it to CJP's Chalo SansadFor many Gen Zers, the road to the Jantar Mantar protest began with a family argument.

She kissed me without consent: IPS trainee accused of harassment attempts suicideA case was registered against Uday Krishna Reddy after a fellow IPS trainee at the National Police Academy accused him of stalking, assault, harassment and secretly recording a private video.

Football's strangest red card? Player sent-off for urinating next to benchBrazilian forward Micael was shown a rare red card before kick-off in the Copa Paulista after urinating beside the substitutes' bench. However, the team did not have to play with a man short.

3 members of Hyderabad family killed in California road crash, 2 critically injuredA Hyderabad family was involved in a fatal road crash in California, leaving three dead and two critically injured. The tragedy has prompted relatives in Saidabad to seek urgent government help for travel, treatment support and repatriation.

Airport, sewage blocking Tamil Nadu investments. Is Vijay up for the challenge?The Niti Aayog's first-ever Investment Friendliness Index ranks Tamil Nadu third in India and praises its strengths. But it also flags areas such as airport capacity, treated sewage water, and port connectivity as key gaps. The challenge now is whether CM Vijay can turn these shortcomings into opportunities and make Tamil Nadu India's most investment-friendly state.

Congress set dangerous precedent by protesting outside PM residence: Govt sourcesThe sources said such protests were "not seen in mature democracies" and warned that demonstrations targeting the residences of political leaders could have serious consequences.

Deeply insensitive: CJP removes Vijeta Dahiya over burger videos during protestThe Cockroach Janta Party removed spokesperson Vijeta Dahiya after a burger outlet video surfaced during its Delhi protest. The party called his conduct deeply insensitive as workers and supporters allegedly faced police action.

Video: CM Vijay's surprise inspection of India's first bio-CNG plant in ChennaiTamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay made a surprise visit to India's first bio-CNG plant in Chennai. He used the inspection to press for stronger scientific waste management and wider replication of such projects across Tamil Nadu.

sports.headline: La Liga chief accuses FIFA of destroying football, calls for Infantino to resign La Liga president Javier Tebas accused FIFA of "destroying the football industry" through the expansion of international competitions and said president Gianni Infantino should resign.

India boxing team hit by luggage chaos ahead of Commonwealth Games campaign India's boxing contingent suffered an early setback to its Commonwealth Games campaign on Tuesday after nearly half the team's luggage failed to arrive from Belfast.

Football's strangest red card? Player sent-off for urinating next to bench Brazilian forward Micael was shown a rare red card before kick-off in the Copa Paulista after urinating beside the substitutes' bench. However, the team did not have to play with a man short.

Chennai Grand Masters: D Gukesh suffers second loss, Sarin beats Erigaise World champion D Gukesh suffered his second consecutive defeat at the Chennai Grand Masters after losing to GM M Pranesh, while GM Nihal Sarin stunned Arjun Erigaisi in the penultimate round on Tuesday.

When in doubt, talk to Ashish Nehra: Ashok Sharma ahead of potential India debut India fast bowler Ashok Sharma credited Gujarat Titans head coach Ashish Nehra for his rapid rise, revealing the former India pacer was always available for advice as he prepares for a likely T20I debut against Zimbabwe.

Rohit Sharma's retirement speculation wasn't merely outside noise: Ashwin Former India spinner Ravichandran Ashwin believes there was substance to the speculation surrounding Rohit Sharma's ODI future. Speaking on his YouTube channel, Ashwin said that there is no smoke without fire.

Pressure is intense: Referee Anthony Taylor retires from football officiating Referee Anthony Taylor has announced his retirement from football officiating with immediate effect, saying the intense pressure and constant scrutiny of the job played a key role in his decision.

Murali Sreeshankar eyes Glasgow gold after strong injury comeback India's premier long jumper Murali Sreeshankar believes his finest performances are still ahead of him as he prepares for the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, saying a "big jump" is around the corner after making an impressive return from a career-threatening injury.

India's recent T20I slump due to overplaying in Asia, says Tilak Varma Vice-captain Tilak Varma addressed India's recent T20I slump during the pre-series press conference in Harare. Varma argued that the team lost all six T20Is in the UK because they spent the majority of the last two years playing in Asia.

BCCI cracks down: Deliberate front-foot no-balls to invite match suspension The BCCI has revised its domestic playing conditions, introducing match-long suspensions for deliberate front-foot no-balls and non-landing deliveries. The changes also include revisions to end-of-day play, final-innings declarations and wicketkeeping rules ahead of the new domestic season.

India tour still on? Bangladesh hopeful despite Mustafizur Rahman IPL saga Bangladesh remains hopeful of hosting India in September and believes sports diplomacy can help ease tensions sparked by the Mustafizur Rahman controversy.

Vinicius Jr. gets face surgery, looks unrecognisable after FIFA World CupBrazil star Vinicius Jr. has reportedly undergone a chin harmonisation procedure after the FIFA World Cup. Images of his transformed appearance are going viral ahead of his return to Real Madrid.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is special, manage him well: Sikandar Raza to IndiaZIM vs IND: Zimbabwe captain Sikandar Raza has urged India to carefully manage teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, backing the 15-year-old to become a generational talent if nurtured properly.

Prannoy, Devika knocked out as India make disappointing China Open startHS Prannoy and Devika Sihag suffered first-round exits at the China Open as India's campaign got off to a disappointing start. PV Sindhu, Lakshya Sen and Ayush Shetty begin their campaigns on Wednesday.

India favourites to win T20I series vs Zimbabwe? Sikandar Raza does not think soZIM vs IND: Zimbabwe captain Sikandar Raza has rejected the notion that India are overwhelming favourites for the upcoming T20I series, saying both teams have an equal chance of winning.

Hockey: India name World Cup squad with a good mix of youth and experienceHockey India have named a 20-member men's World Cup squad led by Harmanpreet Singh, with youth and experience across all lines. India have been drawn alongside Wales, England and Pakistan in the group stages of the tournament, starting August 15.

Want cheap Australian Open tickets? You may have to act faster in 2027The Australian Open will cap allocations of its popular A$49 ground passes during the opening week of the 2027 tournament after crowding and long queues marred this year's event in Melbourne.

Gambhir bashed for KL Rahul call: Like Samson, Sooryavanshi, you screwed him upKrishnamachari Srikkanth criticised Gautam Gambhir and the team management for sending KL Rahul in at No. 6 during India's chase at Lord's. His remarks have revived questions over Rahul's role after India lost the ODI series to England.

Falklands banner backlash: Do Argentina stars face a Premier League ban?Argentina players sparked a row after displaying a Falklands banner following their World Cup win over England. However, any move to revoke Premier League work visas would be highly unlikely.

Thank You, Lionel Messi, for giving the world one last bit of magicLionel Messi may have possibly signed off from the international football like a veteran magician leaving his stage for the final time. During the last two World Cup campaigns, Messi showed how he was able to galvanise the Argentina team for a common goal.

Who needs trophies? Loyal Argentina fans give hero's welcome to losing finalistsArgentina's beaten World Cup finalists arrived in Buenos Aires on Monday. Thousands greeted them with flares, drums and chants, underscoring loyalty that outlasted the missing trophy.

Pat Cummins returns as Australia pick full-strength squad for Bangladesh TestsAUS vs BAN: Pat Cummins, Josh Hazlewood and Nathan Lyon have returned to Australia's 13-man squad for the two-Test World Test Championship series against Bangladesh in Darwin and Mackay.

Inside Spain's World Cup party in Madrid: Footballers turn DJs for 2 million fansSpain's FIFA World Cup winners turned Madrid's Cibeles celebration into a giant DJ-led street party. The homecoming fused state ceremony, football folklore and viral fan moments before nearly two million supporters.

Have FIFA World Cup finals become boring this century?Spain's 1-0 extra-time victory over Argentina brought the curtain down on another unforgettable World Cup. Yet after four weeks of drama, goals and upsets, the final once again struggled to match the spectacle that came before it. Has football's biggest match become the most boring?

PV Sindhu's redemption story began with a phone call to Virat KohliPV Sindhu didn't rebuild her career to win the Japan Open. She rebuilt it to rediscover joy. In Tokyo, beating Akane Yamaguchi for her maiden Super 750 title, she found both. And it all began with a conversation with Virat Kohli.

UTT Season 7: Sathiyan steers Dabang Delhi to crucial win over Ahmedabad APL PipersUTT Season 7: Sathiyan Gnanasekaran guided Dabang Delhi TTC to a 9-6 win over Ahmedabad APL Pipers with victories in the singles and mixed doubles. The result gave Delhi their third win of the campaign and kept them in the playoff race.

Pain is immense: Messi breaks silence after heartbreaking World Cup final defeatLionel Messi has spoken for the first time since Argentina's heartbreaking defeat to Spain in the FIFA World Cup final, admitting the pain of losing the title was "immense" while expressing pride in his team's remarkable run to a second successive final.

FIFA World Cup: Yamal consoled Messi after final, hugs him; moment goes viralWhile emotions ran high after the FIFA World Cup final, one post-match moment stood out above the chaos. As several Argentina and Spain players were involved in heated confrontations following the final whistle, including one Argentine player being sent off, Spain's teenage star Lamine Yamal shared a warm embrace with Lionel Messi. The heartfelt exchange, captured on camera, quickly went viral on social media, with many fans describing it as a symbolic passing of the torch from one generation to the next. Yamal was also seen consoling the Argentine captain in the aftermath of the dramatic contest, offering a striking contrast to the tensions unfolding elsewhere on the pitch. For many viewers, the brief interaction became one of the defining images of the final, highlighting mutual respect and sportsmanship despite the fierce rivalry.

Arjun Erigaisi beats world champion Gukesh with black to jump to joint leadIndia's Arjun Erigaisi outplayed world champion D Gukesh to move into the joint lead after the fifth round of the fourth edition of the Chennai Grand Masters on Monday.

Messi's tears suggest World Cup final was his last for Argentina: Baichung BhutiaIndian football legend Bhaichung Bhutia said he was "sad to see Messi cry" after Argentina's World Cup final defeat to Spain, adding that the tournament could mark the end of Lionel Messi's international career. In an exclusive conversation with Rajdeep Sardesai, Bhutia reflected on Messi's legacy, Argentina's campaign, Spain's World Cup triumph and what the Argentine great has meant to football across generations.

England legend and Liverpool icon Kevin Keegan dies at 75Former Liverpool and England star Kevin Keegan has died at the age of 75 following a battle with cancer. Keegan made 323 appearances for Liverpool between 1971 and 1977, scoring 100 goals.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
